data.body:
## Description
Modify plugins/dso/agents/complexity-evaluator.md to add 3 new output fields:

1. **pattern_familiarity** (high/medium/low): Scoring: High = pattern appears in 2+ existing implementations in this repo; Medium = pattern is common in the ecosystem but novel to this repo; Low = novel pattern with no precedent in this repo or ecosystem. Agent must search repo history and existing skills before scoring.

2. **external_boundary_count** (integer): Count of external systems/tools/APIs/services the ticket interacts with. Zero external boundaries is a strong signal against COMPLEX.

3. **feasibility_review_recommended** (boolean): True when external_boundary_count > 0 OR pattern_familiarity is Low. This signals to brainstorm that a feasibility reviewer should be triggered.

All changes are additive — preserve all existing fields, behavior, and classification logic. The new fields appear in the Output Schema JSON block alongside existing fields.

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/agents/complexity-evaluator.md — Edit

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Output Schema includes pattern_familiarity field
  Verify: grep -q 'pattern_familiarity' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/agents/complexity-evaluator.md
- [ ] Output Schema includes external_boundary_count field
  Verify: grep -q 'external_boundary_count' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/agents/complexity-evaluator.md
- [ ] Output Schema includes feasibility_review_recommended field
  Verify: grep -q 'feasibility_review_recommended' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/agents/complexity-evaluator.md
- [ ] Existing output schema fields preserved
  Verify: grep -q '"classification"' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/agents/complexity-evaluator.md && grep -q '"confidence"' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/agents/complexity-evaluator.md
- [ ] All tests pass
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && poetry run pytest tests/skills/test_complexity_evaluator_agent.py --tb=short -q

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent.
